folio.l: perils ,

and it happetú outherwhile , æ as sone is the grete man
slayn , as the litel man

¢ And as it is I wryten , in the secund
book of kynges ; the dedes of batailles ben auenturouse , and no
thyngö certeyne
¢ for as ligútly is oon hurt w a spere , as anoæä

And for æäe is gret ¹il in werre , æäfore shulde a man flee , and
eschue werre , in as muchel , as a man may , goodly

¢ For Sala-
mon seitú ; he æ louetú ¹il , shal falle in ¹ile ,

¢ After æat
Dame Pèdence had spoken in this manere ,, Melibe answe-
red and seide

¢ I se wel Dame Pèdence , that be ©oure faire
wordes , & by ©oure resons æ ©e han shewed me , æ the werre li-
ketú ©ow no thyngö ,

but I haue nat ©et herd ©oure cou~seil , how
I shal do in this nede

¢ Certes q¶ she , I cou~seile ©ow , æ ©e
acorde w ©oure aduäsaries , & æ ©e haue pees w he~ ,,

For Sei~t
Iame seitú in hise Epistles ; æ by concord and pees , æe sma-
le richesses wexen grete ,

& by discord and debate , the grete ri-
chesses fallen doun

¢ And ©e knowen wel , æ oon of the gretteste
and moost souäeyn thyngö , æ is in this world , is vnyte and
pees

¢ And æäfore seide oure lord Ihu~ Cést to hise Apostlees ,
in this wyse ,

wel happy & blissed be they , æ louen & påchasen
pees , for they ben called , children of god

¢ A quod Melibee ,
now se I wel , æ ©e louen nat myn honoå , ne my worshipe ,

ñe knowen wel , æ myne aduäsaries , han bigo~ne this debat
& bryge , by here outge ,

& ©e se wel , æ æei ne requeren , ne
preye me nat of pees , ne they asken nat to be reconsiled ,

wol ©e tha~ne æ I go & meke me & obeye me to he~ , & crie he~
mäcy ;
For sothe æ were nat my worshipe ,

For rigút as
men seyn , æ ouer greet homlynesse , engendretú dispreisynge ,
So faretú it , by to greet humylitee , or mekenesse

¢ Tha~ne
bygan Dame Prudence , to maken semblant of wratthe , &
seide ,

Certes sire , sauf ©oure gce , I loue ©oure honoå , and ©oåe
¸fit , as I do myn owene , & euäe haue doon ,

ne ©e , ne noon
other , seyn neuäe the contrie ,

And if I had seid , æ ©e shul-
de han påchased the pees , & the reconsiliacou~ , I ne hadde
nat muchel mys take me , ne seid amys

For the wyse
ma~ seitú ; The dissencou~ bigynnetú by another man , and
the reconsilynge , bigynnetú by thy self

¢ And the ¸phete
seitú ; Flee shrewednesse , & do goodnesse ,

seke pees & folwe
it , as muchel as in the is

¢ ñet seie I nat , æ ©e shuln ra-
there påsue to ©oure aduäsaries for pees , than they shuln to
©ow ,

For I knowe wel , that ©e ben so hard herted , æ ©e wol
do noo thyngö for me

¢ And Salamon seitú ; æ he æ hatú
ouä hard an herte , atte laste , he shal myshappe & mystide
,, Whan Melibee had herd Dame Pèdence , make semblau~t
